column2: EPIC Malekith the Accursed Crashes the Moon into the Sun
column23: Well, Shapeshifting Symbiote and Doctor of Beatdown 'saved the day'. The tech/strength bystander deck really did the work. Although Ruby/Mirage also gave punches that gave you (waking) nightmares. I also was lucky with that deadly 9th twist being two turns away before ending the game. Second game went on longer, had more VP, but the points per turn were a little lower. That game had a better strenght/tech deck, but missing the Save the Day got me less bystanders.

Couldn't resist going the discard route. Early master strikes and weapons being ko'd + Kylah recruiting Ruby's rare on turn 5 + getting Stormbreaker at appropriate times just made for a smooth ride. (Not to mention the lucky top deck bystander when we thought we were about to have an escape)

I initially though a discard deck with beta reta bill and ruby summers would be the winning deck, unfortunately I used Bill last week. I went with spiders. Silk is great. Save the day from Spider-Gwen generated a lot of attack as long as I could keep the rooftops cleared,  which was easier thanks to villainous weapons not pushing. There were a lot of bystanders in this scenario. I think of the 35 I rescued 20 of them were normal. I ended up failing 3 sunlight orbits because I couldn't recruit enough spiders, (silk and gwen don't make recruit).

This setup was really cool, my first attempt was disaster due to too much grey Maria Hills, that's why I decided to play it again. Second try was much better, Left was focusing on Mirage/Wolfsbane and Right was leading an All-Inhumans team. Mirage really helped with the waking nightmares and she generated crazy amount of attack with her uncommon (with the help of Wolfsbane of course) that was enough to clear the city on several occasions. Right was finally getting enough attack to fight Emma Frost twice in a row and I hit her "Diamond form" tactic that costed me 2 additional turns for the final victory. Overall the setup was super fun and secretly challenging with the scheme.

The 2nd game I ended up with so much KO power. There was only one starting card in either deck left at the end. Emma Frost at that point didnt stand a chance. Was most happy that the right hand side managed to get a deck based on Crystal working as when that gets going she hits hard.

Between scheme, circus of crime, Black bolt, Medusa, and Wolfsbane so much ko of Grey's. Mirage took care of the Grey cards that ended up in my hand. Could have ended the game a turn early except for the card that wouldn't let me hit Emma until the start of my next turn. So excited to choose and play with 2 of my top 5 heroes.
I got hq locked by 4 rares, but both sides managed to ko down to "hit mm every turn" by that point.

We had the same number of turns with both attempts, but they were wildly different games. The first seemed like we were more powerful sooner, but the second we had more victory points so the one that was a little less fun was the one that ended up being the one we counted. Hoping we make the top ten, but if not... best of luck to everyone else! Thanks for letting us play in the tournament!

Built a Hyperspeed deck on the right, and a Thrones Favor deck on the left. Both worked like a charm!! This scheme matched with Emma Frost was absolutely brilliant!! Very tricky, timing wise, but super fun when you figure it out!!
I forgot how much I love Apocalypse! Got lucky with a couple early Mr Sinister clones, which allowed me to rack up on receipt and then get some big cards early (thank you, Star Lord).

This is the stuff of nightmares people, Veiled scheme KO's non grey, Mastermind Tactics KO's non grey, Even vilains KO,s non grey (Death), and once Unveiled scheme hits you are already halfway to loss

This game was going well, very efficient pairing of Bishop and Jean Grey and only 1 stolen cerebro data. I went for villians over Apocalypse a few times ... then boy did the game draw out after a few Master Stikes hit close together. Hoping high VP makes up for my escapes and turn count.

To use up Iceman only to have him not show up in the Hero Deck… ugh. Apocalypse continues to be our foil in these tournaments, and we got Slamwich’d twice (Strike-other card-Strike) that crippled us for 4 straight turns each time. A setup we would love another crack at…

Oof, deck thinning played right into late game master strikes, ended the first game before the scheme transformation due to horsemen escapes, turns out the master strikes in the first half was almost better than later, poor player 1 was skipped three times, both were skipped twice. Didn't help that I'd ko'd both Boom-booms before the flip, as well as a couple of the rocket cards that weren't working out due to the low guardian count.
